records.ety: [L. limitatio: cf. F. limitation. See Limit, v. t.]

defs: The act of limiting; the state or condition of being limited; as, the limitation of his authority was approved by the council.
quotes: They had no right to mistake the limitation . . . of their own faculties, for an inherent limitation of the possible modes of existence in the universe.

defs: That which limits; a restriction; a qualification; a restraining condition, defining circumstance, or qualifying conception; as, limitations of thought.
quotes: The cause of error is ignorance what restraints and limitations all principles have in regard of the matter whereunto they are applicable.

defs: A certain precinct within which friars were allowed to beg, or exercise their functions; also, the time during which they were permitted to exercise their functions in such a district.
A limited time within or during which something is to be done.
quotes: You have stood your limitation, and the tribunes Endue you with the people's voice.

defs: A certain period limited by statute after which the claimant shall not enforce his claims by suit.
A settling of an estate or property by specific rules.
A restriction of power; as, a constitutional limitation.
